name: Dolmen del Prado de Las Cruces — circular desde Ávila por la Cañada Real Soriana Occidental
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ávila traverse a diverse landscape, characterized by the rugged peaks of the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park and the gentler terrain of the River Tormes valleys. The region, situated at 3714 feet (1132 meters) above sea level, features challenging mountain passes, extensive dehesa landscapes, and ancient forests. Its varied topography, influenced by the Sistema Central mountain range, offers a wide array of terrain for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Ávila The most popular touring cycling route is Dolmen del Prado de Las Cruces — circular desde Ávila por la Cañada Real Soriana Occidental, a 12.1 miles (19.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores historical elements and rural paths. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Southeast Route, an easy 3.7 miles (6.0 km) path. This shorter route offers a gentle ride through the local scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Ávila City Walls – Avila Cathedral loop from Ávila, a 26.0 miles (41.8 km) trail leading through the historic city center and past the UNESCO World Heritage site, often completed in about 3 hours 28 minutes. Touring cycling around Ávila is defined by high-altitude mountain ranges, serene river valleys, and historic cityscapes, including the Walls of Ávila. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to demanding climbs. The routes in Ávila are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ávila's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Ávila?
A: Ávila offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 100 options available. These routes cater to various preferences, from leisurely rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels for touring cycling in Ávila?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ávila are diverse, with options for all skill levels. You'll find approximately 8 easy routes, around 41 moderate routes, and about 55 difficult routes, ensuring there's a suitable challenge for every cyclist.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Ávila?
A: The komoot community highly rates the touring cycling experience in Ávila, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 13 reviews. More than 800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the region, often praising the varied terrain and stunning views.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling in Ávila?
A: Ávila's touring cycling routes traverse a diverse landscape. You'll encounter the rugged peaks and high-altitude valleys of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Gredos Regional Park&lt;/strong&gt;, serene &lt;strong&gt;River Tormes valleys&lt;/strong&gt;, characteristic &lt;strong&gt;dehesa landscapes&lt;/strong&gt;, and extensive chestnut and oak forests. The region's position within the Sistema Central mountain range ensures varied topography and expansive panoramic views.

Q: Are there historical sites or landmarks I can visit along touring cycling routes in Ávila?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Ávila pass by significant historical and cultural attractions. You can cycle past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4435034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls of Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, or explore charming ancient villages. Other notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5797464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Roman bridge over the Adaja River&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1034227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Basilica of San Vicente of Ávila&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ávila?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a gentle introduction to touring cycling in Ávila, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1105202555/southeast-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Southeast Route&lt;/a&gt; offer an easy ride through local scenery. There are approximately 8 easy routes available, perfect for beginners or a relaxed day out.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Ávila?
A: Experienced cyclists will find plenty of demanding routes in Ávila, especially within the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Gredos Regional Park&lt;/strong&gt;, which features rugged peaks and significant elevation gains. The region's high altitude and mountain passes like Navalmoral Pass and Mijares Pass provide a true test of endurance. One challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10412848" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ávila City Walls – Avila Cathedral loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 40 km with considerable ascent.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Ávila?
A: Yes, Ávila offers several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1111737853/dolmen-del-prado-de-las-cruces-circular-desde-avila-por-la-canada-real-soriana-occidental" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen del Prado de Las Cruces — circular desde Ávila por la Cañada Real Soriana Occidental&lt;/a&gt;, which explores historical elements and rural paths. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33911213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo Azálvaro Bike Path – Campo Azálvaro Route loop from Ávila&lt;/a&gt;, offering a moderate challenge.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints along touring cycling routes in Ávila?
A: Ávila is known for its breathtaking vistas. Cyclists can enjoy panoramic views from spots like Mirador del Circo de Gredos and Cruz del Herrerito. Just outside the city, the &lt;strong&gt;Cuatro Postes (Four Posts)&lt;/strong&gt; monument offers an iconic view of Ávila and its medieval walls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5621589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Canchal&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent viewpoint to consider.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Ávila?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Ávila is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be hot, especially in lower elevations, while winters can be cold with snow in the higher mountain areas like the Sierra de Gredos.

Q: Are there routes that explore the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes venture into the majestic &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Gredos Regional Park&lt;/strong&gt;. This area is characterized by rugged peaks, high-altitude valleys, and demanding mountain passes, offering spectacular scenery and challenging climbs for cyclists seeking an adventure in the mountains.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through traditional villages?
A: Absolutely. Touring cycling routes in Ávila frequently pass through ancient, picturesque villages, each with its unique charm. You might cycle through places like El Barco de Ávila, known for its 12th-century walls, or Candelario, celebrated for its traditional stone houses. These villages offer a glimpse into the region's rich history and culture.

Q: Are there any routes that incorporate ancient roads or historical paths?
A: Yes, some touring cycling routes in Ávila incorporate segments of ancient Roman roads and the historic &lt;strong&gt;Vía de la Plata&lt;/strong&gt; pilgrimage route. These paths add a fascinating layer of historical discovery to your cycling journey, allowing you to ride through centuries of history.

This dolmen has an ancient history, being used as a burial place from the end of the Neolithic to the beginning of the Bronze Age, covering the last centuries of the fourth millennium BC. until the first third of the second millennium BC. Its presence evokes mystery and connects us with the ancient cultures that built it.

waypoints.name: Dolmen del Prado de Las Cruces
